# Resizable

Builds adjustable horizontal or vertical application layouts with keyboard support.

## Installation

Add Resizable from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/resizable
```

## Usage

Place panels and handles as direct siblings inside a `ResizablePanelGroup`. Its required `direction` controls both layout and resize behavior. Give the group a measurable height when using vertical direction.

```vue
<template>
  <ResizablePanelGroup direction="horizontal" class="min-h-48 max-w-md rounded-lg border">
    <ResizablePanel :default-size="50">
      <div class="flex h-full items-center justify-center p-6">Navigation</div>
    </ResizablePanel>
    <ResizableHandle with-handle />
    <ResizablePanel :default-size="50">
      <div class="flex h-full items-center justify-center p-6">Content</div>
    </ResizablePanel>
  </ResizablePanelGroup>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  ResizableHandle,
  ResizablePanel,
  ResizablePanelGroup,
} from '@/Components/Resizable'
</script>
```

## Vertical layout

Vertical groups work well for editor previews, consoles, and other stacked workspaces. The visible grip is optional; set `with-handle` only when the stronger affordance helps users discover the divider.

**Vertical example**

```vue
<template>
  <ResizablePanelGroup direction="vertical" class="min-h-64 max-w-md rounded-lg border">
    <ResizablePanel :default-size="60" :min-size="30">
      <div class="flex h-full items-center justify-center p-6">Preview</div>
    </ResizablePanel>
    <ResizableHandle with-handle />
    <ResizablePanel :default-size="40" :min-size="20">
      <div class="flex h-full items-center justify-center p-6">Console</div>
    </ResizablePanel>
  </ResizablePanelGroup>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  ResizableHandle,
  ResizablePanel,
  ResizablePanelGroup,
} from '@/Components/Resizable'
</script>
```

## Panel constraints

Use `default-size` to establish the initial proportions and `min-size`/`max-size` to protect usable content. Panels also support collapsible layouts with `collapsible` and `collapsed-size`. Values use percentages by default; `size-unit="px"` is available when a fixed boundary is truly required.

For conditionally rendered panels, provide stable ids and explicit order. The group can persist a layout with `auto-save-id`, or the parent can observe the `layout` event and own persistence.

## Responsive behavior

Resizing is not a substitute for responsive design. On small screens, consider switching direction, hiding a secondary panel behind navigation, or rendering a single active pane. Keep minimum sizes large enough for focusable controls and translated labels.

## Accessibility

The Reka splitter handle supports pointer and keyboard resizing and receives focus styles. Do not remove the handle from the tab order unless the layout is genuinely fixed. Give each pane its own heading or landmark so users can understand the regions independently of size.

## When to use

- Use Resizable for tool-like workspaces where users benefit from allocating space.
- Avoid it for ordinary page columns or cards that should follow responsive CSS automatically.
- Use Scroll Area inside a pane only when that pane also has an intentional bounded height.

Hosted documentation: [Resizable](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/resizable)
